title: Astragalus mazama
intro: Astragalus mazama, also known as Mazama's milkvetch, is a perennial plant species in the pea family (Fabaceae) native to the western United States and Canada. The root of Astragalus mazama has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to provide adaptogenic and immune-boosting properties, supporting the body's ability to resist stress and infection, as well as to alleviate symptoms of fatigue and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Astragalus mazama include triterpene saponins, flavonoids, and polysaccharides, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory activities. Astragalus mazama is often prepared as a decoction, capsule, or tincture, and is commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine to enhance overall health and vitality, as well as to support the immune system during illnesses such as the common cold and flu.
taxonomy: Astragalus mazama is classified as a species of legume in the family Fabaceae. This classification is based on the plant's botanical characteristics and genetic relationships. More specifically, Astragalus mazama is a member of the genus Astragalus, which comprises over 2,000 species of vetches and milkvetches. The plant's classification is further refined to the subfamily Faboideae, which includes a wide range of legumes. Astragalus mazama's classification is used in botanical and horticultural contexts to identify and distinguish the species from other related plants.
uses: Astragalus mazama, also known as Mazama's milkvetch, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine. Traditionally, it has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant's roots and leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce swelling and ease pain. Additionally, Astragalus mazama is believed to have immunomodulatory effects, potentially helping to boost the body's immune system. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential benefits.

side_effects: Astragalus mazama is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine, but there is limited research on its specific side effects.
